text: Large Language Models, or LLMs, are advanced AI systems that understand and generate human-like text. They work by analyzing vast amounts of written material, such as books, articles, and websites. This training data helps the model learn patterns in language, so it can predict and form coherent responses. When you use an LLM, you provide a prompt—a question or instruction. This is called prompt engineering, and a clear, specific prompt often leads to better answers. LLMs can write essays, summarize information, or even create stories. However, they have limits. They may produce incorrect or biased information, and they do not truly understand meaning—they only mimic patterns from their training.
